target: Section 1.6 Exhibit A in data/sections.json part1 — append new paragraph before the closing of the Exhibit A subsection (before '<h3>Evidence Exhibit B')
replacement_html: The dome's graph raises deeper questions. The homepage features a prominent "Forensic Zoom" figure for the Oct 30, 2003 event (00:55–01:22 UTC) showing BOU magnetic variance (top panel) and what is labeled "Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (μGal)" vs. "Level-1B Corrected Administrative Mask" (bottom panel). The top panel's BOU magnetic data is plausible — real INTERMAGNET BOU data for this window shows H-component variations of ~32 nT and F-component variations of ~100 nT, consistent with the graph's scale. But the bottom panel has multiple critical problems. First, GRACE measures inter-satellite K-band range changes, not local gravity in μGal — the "μGal" unit belongs to terrestrial superconducting gravimeters, not satellite gravimetry. Second, no GRACE data product called "Raw Induction Residual" exists in any JPL or GFZ documentation. Third, GRACE orbited at ~500 km altitude at 7.5 km/s, covering ~13,500 km of ground track in the 27-minute window — it was not stationary over Boulder for co-temporal comparison with a ground magnetometer. Fourth, GRACE-A entered safe mode during the Halloween 2003 storm (documented by Sutton et al., 2005; Bruinsma et al., 2006), making accelerometer data unreliable or absent for this period. The red "L1B Corrected Administrative Mask" trend line appears manually drawn and flat — actual L1B processing (JPL D-22027) applies calibrations that do not flatten signals in this manner. In sum, the bottom panel conflates satellite and terrestrial measurement types, uses terminology that matches no real data product, and collocates a fast-moving satellite with a fixed ground station. The graph appears to illustrate a narrative rather than real GRACE data.
insertion_point: Insert this paragraph immediately before 'Evidence Exhibit B' — i.e., at the end of the Exhibit A subsection, after the paragraph about r=0.992 and AI=1.152 being hardcoded HTML.
holes_addressed: Graph forensics: dome's 'Forensic Zoom' figure uses fabricated GRACE terminology
μGal unit mismatch: GRACE doesn't measure local gravity in μGal
No GRACE data product called 'Raw Induction Residual'
GRACE orbital velocity makes ground-station collocation impossible in 27-min window
GRACE-A safe mode during Halloween 2003 storm documented in peer-reviewed literature
L1B 'Administrative Mask' trend line appears manually drawn
new_evidence_added: Real BOU INTERMAGNET data verified: H range ~32 nT, F range ~100 nT in 00:55-01:22 window — top panel is plausible
Sutton et al. 2005 and Bruinsma et al. 2006 document GRACE-A safe mode during this storm
JPL D-22027 is the GRACE L1B processing specification
GRACE ground speed calculation: 7.5 km/s × 1620s = 12,150 km ground track
anticipated_objections: Dome claims 'induction residual' is their own derived quantity → Still labeled as 'Level-1A' which is a specific NASA data product designation; if it's a dome derivation, calling it L1A is misleading
Dome claims data was post-processed from raw downloads → No processing code exists in the repository (already documented)
Dome claims safe mode was brief → Published literature documents extended safe mode during this storm; accelerometer data is unreliable regardless of exact duration
kernel_of_truth: The top panel of the dome's graph does appear to use real BOU magnetometer data — the scale and variability are consistent with actual INTERMAGNET records for this extreme event. The Halloween 2003 storm really was extraordinary and did produce remarkable multi-instrument correlations. The dome correctly identifies this as an interesting scientific event. The problem is specifically with the bottom panel, which appears to use fabricated or mislabeled data to support the correlation claim.
